name: Vaccinia_WR_full_genome
description: Vaccinia virus (Western Reserve strain) complete linear dsDNA genome ~194 kb with covalently-closed hairpin termini and identical inverted terminal repeats (ITRs) of ~10 kb at each end. The genome encodes ~200 ORFs (the central region is highly conserved across all poxviruses and encodes core replication/transcription machinery; the terminal regions carry host-range, immune-evasion, and virulence factors). Replicates entirely in the cytoplasm using viral-encoded RNA polymerase, DNA polymerase, and capping enzymes — no nuclear involvement. The covalently-closed AT-rich hairpin termini (flip/flop A and B isomers) are the substrate for resolution during replication.
expected_copy_count: 1 input genome per virion; 10000+ progeny per infected cell
